goal: is_monotone_on[Domain, Value](domain, f) = forall(x0: Domain, x1: Domain) { domain.contains(x0) and domain.contains(x1) and x0 <= x1 implies f(x0) <= f(x1) }
proof: function[T0: PartialOrder, T2: PartialOrder](x0: Set[T0], x1: T0 -> T2) { forall(x2: T0, x3: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or not x3 ∈ x0 or not x2 <= x3 or x1(x2) <= x1(x3) } = is_monotone_on[T0, T2](x0, x1) }[Domain, Value](domain, f)

goal: domain.contains(left) and domain.contains(right) and left <= right implies f(left) <= f(right)
proof: is_monotone_on[Domain, Value](domain, f) and left ∈ domain and right ∈ domain
not is_monotone_on[Domain, Value](domain, f) or forall(x0: Domain, x1: Domain) { not x0 ∈ domain or not x1 ∈ domain or not x0 <= x1 or f(x0) <= f(x1) } = true
is_monotone_on[Domain, Value](domain, f)
function(x0: Domain) { forall(x1: Domain) { not x0 ∈ domain or not x1 ∈ domain or not x0 <= x1 or f(x0) <= f(x1) } = true }(left)
function(x0: Domain, x1: Domain) { not (x0 ∈ domain and x1 ∈ domain and x0 <= x1 and not f(x0) <= f(x1)) }(left, right)

goal: is_antitone_on[Domain, Value](domain, f) = forall(x0: Domain, x1: Domain) { domain.contains(x0) and domain.contains(x1) and x0 <= x1 implies f(x1) <= f(x0) }
proof: function[T0: PartialOrder, T2: PartialOrder](x0: Set[T0], x1: T0 -> T2) { forall(x2: T0, x3: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or not x3 ∈ x0 or not x2 <= x3 or x1(x3) <= x1(x2) } = is_antitone_on[T0, T2](x0, x1) }[Domain, Value](domain, f)

goal: domain.contains(left) and domain.contains(right) and left <= right implies f(right) <= f(left)
proof: is_antitone_on[Domain, Value](domain, f) and left ∈ domain and right ∈ domain
not is_antitone_on[Domain, Value](domain, f) or forall(x0: Domain, x1: Domain) { not x0 ∈ domain or not x1 ∈ domain or not x0 <= x1 or f(x1) <= f(x0) } = true

function(x0: Domain) { forall(x1: Domain) { not x0 ∈ domain or not x1 ∈ domain or not x0 <= x1 or f(x1) <= f(x0) } = true }(left)
function(x0: Domain, x1: Domain) { not (x0 ∈ domain and x1 ∈ domain and x0 <= x1 and not f(x1) <= f(x0)) }(left, right)

proof: function[T0: PartialOrder, T2: PartialOrder](x0: Set[T0], x1: T0 -> T2) { forall(x2: T0, x3: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or not x3 ∈ x0 or not x2 < x3 or x1(x2) < x1(x3) } = is_strict_monotone_on[T0, T2](x0, x1) }[Domain, Value](domain, f)

goal: domain.contains(left) and domain.contains(right) and left < right implies f(left) < f(right)
proof: is_strict_monotone_on[Domain, Value](domain, f) and left ∈ domain and right ∈ domain

function[T0: PartialOrder, T2: PartialOrder](x0: Set[T0], x1: T0 -> T2) { forall(x2: T0, x3: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or not x3 ∈ x0 or not x2 <= x3 or x1(x2) <= x1(x3) } = is_monotone_on[T0, T2](x0, x1) }[Domain, Value](domain, f)
let w0: Domain satisfy { exists(k0: Domain) { w0 ∈ domain and k0 ∈ domain and w0 <= k0 and not f(w0) <= f(k0) } }
let w1: Domain satisfy { w0 ∈ domain and w1 ∈ domain and w0 <= w1 and not f(w0) <= f(w1) }
let w2: Domain satisfy { w0 ∈ domain and w2 ∈ domain and w0 <= w2 and not f(w0) <= f(w2) }
function[T0: PartialOrder, T1: PartialOrder](x0: T0 -> T1, x1: T0, x2: T0) { not is_monotone[T0, T1](x0) or not x1 <= x2 or x0(x1) <= x0(x2) }[Domain, Value](f, w0, w2)

function[T0: PartialOrder, T2: PartialOrder](x0: Set[T0], x1: T0 -> T2) { forall(x2: T0, x3: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or not x3 ∈ x0 or not x2 <= x3 or x1(x3) <= x1(x2) } = is_antitone_on[T0, T2](x0, x1) }[Domain, Value](domain, f)
